[2018-04-04 21:29] Deleted User (Deleted User): seems u just click every turn no?
[2018-04-04 21:29] darkeisbein: if u do that it's just lackluster
[2018-04-04 21:30] darkeisbein: if you click it every turn it attacks for 2 and constructs two gauss charges when bought
[2018-04-04 21:30] .bky_1556 (bky): You're only slightly overpaying for 2 permanent damage plus 2 more first turn burst
[2018-04-04 21:30] .bky_1556 (bky): but you should be getting more of a bulk discount given its big tech requirement.
[2018-04-04 21:31] iminabearsuit: Holding it back to block is similar value to Lancetooth. With the upside of synergies.
[2018-04-04 21:32] darkeisbein: it's a unit that is probably only really good with synergy
[2018-04-04 21:32] darkeisbein: but there is lot's of synergy
[2018-04-04 21:32] iminabearsuit: The unit is really strong. But can be very clunky to get out.
[2018-04-04 21:32] darkeisbein: is it really strong though? 2 attack + 2 gauss charges seems meh
[2018-04-04 21:32] darkeisbein: for how clunky it is
[2018-04-04 21:33] darkeisbein: like yeah you get 2 attack for 10BA I guess
[2018-04-04 21:33] darkeisbein: which is efficient but not crazy efficient
[2018-04-04 21:33] .bky_1556 (bky): It's worth holding for 1 granularity exploit, too.
[2018-04-04 21:33] extratricky: we don't really want to be buying gauss charges at 1G either though
[2018-04-04 21:33] darkeisbein: and probably it's slightly less efficient cause gauss charge isn't all that efficient
[2018-04-04 21:33] iminabearsuit: Once you see it in action with bloodragers and iso, it's super strong.
[2018-04-04 21:34] darkeisbein: yeah sure with synergy it's really strong
[2018-04-04 21:34] darkeisbein: but you need synergy units to make it work
[2018-04-04 21:34] iminabearsuit: Like it couldn't be any cheaper than it is ATM.
[2018-04-04 21:35] iminabearsuit: Most of the luminary thought it looked super busted in the current state. But after playing with it, it felt fine.
[2018-04-04 21:36] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I like Valk, really happy with the new units so far and the balance patch.
[2018-04-04 21:36] darkeisbein: I just don't think you ever get this without synergy unless you really want 2 gauss charges on a turn
[2018-04-04 21:38] f300xen (f0x): it's basically arms race
[2018-04-04 21:38] darkeisbein: it's not armsrace though
[2018-04-04 21:38] mtanzer: it looks like it's on the weak side in a vacuum but really strong in certain types of sets to me, pretty much what others are saying right?
[2018-04-04 21:38] redzonex (Redzone): I agree with that assessment
[2018-04-04 21:40] darkeisbein: also super tricky, this looks like a unit that takes a lot of prismata skills to utilize properly
[2018-04-04 21:41] mtanzer: what's so tricky about it?  choosing whether to click it or not seems easy enough
[2018-04-04 21:41] darkeisbein: well I guess some synergy like cryo ray is easy
[2018-04-04 21:41] mtanzer: perhaps setting up your build to get the most value out of it will take some thinking ahead
[2018-04-04 21:41] darkeisbein: but all the syncing and denying absorb and exploiting stuff is kinda hard
[2018-04-04 21:41] darkeisbein: and it's needs some setup in the build aswell
[2018-04-04 21:43] mtanzer: I think in most sets you won't get a lot of turns where you actually deny absorb but then again maybe it just doesn't get bought in a lot of those
[2018-04-04 21:43] darkeisbein: seems really bad vs. vores/cauterizer 😄 how are you exploitin that

[2018-04-04 22:03] Deleted User (Deleted User): any proper deemer chief set is a p2 autowin sort of
[2018-04-04 22:05] Deleted User (Deleted User): so redrame really thinks valky is too stronk?
[2018-04-04 22:06] iminabearsuit: As I said, most luminaries thought it was too strong.
[2018-04-04 22:07] iminabearsuit: Before playing with it*
[2018-04-04 22:07] redrame: I had only heard rumors about valky being too strong, and then I played it and it was worse than that
[2018-04-04 22:08] redrame: it's not gonna break every game but it's gonna make some pretty degenerate ones
[2018-04-04 22:08] Deleted User (Deleted User): it seems like a t5 unit at best
[2018-04-04 22:08] iminabearsuit: Some degeneracy is fine from time to time.
[2018-04-04 22:08] Deleted User (Deleted User): degenerate ones like cryo?
[2018-04-04 22:08] redrame: cryo is the low hanging fruit
[2018-04-04 22:08] iminabearsuit: Cryo is honestly the least degenerate.
[2018-04-04 22:08] redrame: it's pretty degen with thermite too
[2018-04-04 22:08] iminabearsuit: Thermite Bloodrager and lancetooth are the dangerous ones.
[2018-04-04 22:09] redrame: like you need 2 thermites to anti-absorb a valk now
[2018-04-04 22:09] mrguy888: everything is degen with thermite now though
[2018-04-04 22:09] redrame: and they get barriers that do nothing
[2018-04-04 22:09] iminabearsuit: Yeah. It needed like 3 lifespan max.
[2018-04-04 22:09] Deleted User (Deleted User): sorry i'm not following completely how would u play the line
[2018-04-04 22:09] redrame: 5 ls really does hurt it a bit
[2018-04-04 22:09] redrame: but valk thermite is over the top
[2018-04-04 22:09] Deleted User (Deleted User): oh basically denying the barrier absorb?
[2018-04-04 22:09] redrame: you deny regular and barrier
[2018-04-04 22:10] iminabearsuit: Denying 4 absorb, is better than doing 2 damage.
[2018-04-04 22:10] redrame: I would say thermite>cryo>tooth>rager on the degen list
[2018-04-04 22:10] Deleted User (Deleted User): the simple solution is to give opp husks and make it cheaper or some shit
[2018-04-04 22:10] redrame: arka valk is also kinda nuts
[2018-04-04 22:10] iminabearsuit: Nah, the unit is interesting because of barriers.
[2018-04-04 22:10] darkeisbein: iso and immos and scorchs are on there aswell right?
[2018-04-04 22:10] iminabearsuit: Barriers makes you want to break it.
[2018-04-04 22:10] redrame: the problem is just in the design
[2018-04-04 22:11] iminabearsuit: And breaking units is fun.
[2018-04-04 22:11] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I'm amazed by how old timers of the game talk about the balance of the units. Makes me feel like RNG (p1 vs p2) has a lot more weight than advertised.
[2018-04-04 22:11] darkeisbein: nah it's being oversold
[2018-04-04 22:11] redrame: old timers typically select those cases to talk about
[2018-04-04 22:11] redrame: that being said it does get a little more significant as you move up
[2018-04-04 22:12] Deleted User (Deleted User): like gwent coinflip!
[2018-04-04 22:12] iminabearsuit: Player advantage can be upwards of 150 mmr or something I would say.
[2018-04-04 22:12] redrame: if you pick a different strategy than your opponent the better strategy pretty much always wins without player position saying anything about it
[2018-04-04 22:12] mrguy888: It isn't nearly as bad as coinflip on average.
[2018-04-04 22:12] redrame: but better players are more likely to mirror
[2018-04-04 22:12] redrame: yeah it's nowhere close to coinflip but that's a pretty low bar
[2018-04-04 22:13] Deleted User (Deleted User): yeah I meant getting more significant as u move up
[2018-04-04 22:13] mrguy888: Coinflip in Prismata does decide games though.
[2018-04-04 22:13] Deleted User (Deleted User): p adv being on avg like 30-40 or so MMR seems reasonable?
[2018-04-04 22:13] redrame: coinflip is very likely to decide a game if you dont see it coming
[2018-04-04 22:14] redrame: usually there's something you can do to defeat whatever the easy line is
[2018-04-04 22:14] redrame: sometimes there just isnt
[2018-04-04 22:14] mrguy888: Like vs a good player you can make it a close loss but it is still a loss.
[2018-04-04 22:15] Deleted User (Deleted User): what is the avg MMR difference between losing and winning the coinflip
[2018-04-04 22:15] redrame: I would say cf doesn't matter in an easily detectable way in somewhere between 80 and 90 percent of games

[2018-04-04 22:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): is fuge a p adv set?
[2018-04-04 22:36] darkeisbein: depends on what else is in the set right?
[2018-04-04 22:36] redrame: old fuge is typically p2 favored or pretty balanced
[2018-04-04 22:37] redrame: you'll find sets where it's p1 favored
[2018-04-04 22:37] extratricky: in my game history zemora helps p2 but it's not a particularly large effect
[2018-04-04 22:38] redrame: if 2nd fuge is an advantage then it's usually a fairly interesting unit but if it's a disadvantage it just goes super degen
[2018-04-04 22:38] xyotsuba: New fuge seems better for p1
[2018-04-04 22:38] redrame: the idea behind new fuge is that the timings are more complex
[2018-04-04 22:38] xyotsuba: It fits perfectly in every build
[2018-04-04 22:38] extratricky: I think new fuge will have more skill input than old fuge
[2018-04-04 22:38] redrame: so even if you find sets where it's favored you have options
[2018-04-04 22:39] redrame: it prob is a bit p1 favored yeah
[2018-04-04 22:39] xyotsuba: dd d1 —� dd dd dd1 — dd dde ddd ddd1
[2018-04-04 22:40] xyotsuba: P2 makes it after conduit if they wanna fit it perfectly
[2018-04-04 22:40] extratricky: sure blue sets generally favor p1 now because b fits better, so I guess new fuge might turn out similar
[2018-04-04 22:40] extratricky: but it's not a large effect at all
[2018-04-04 22:40] redrame: floating 2 to get your preferred fuge timing is nothing compared to losing to earliest possible fuge in terms of creating problems though
[2018-04-04 22:40] mtanzer: don't forget 1.  1  😄
[2018-04-04 22:40] redrame: 1. 1 is a risk with wincer and not much else
[2018-04-04 22:40] redrame: most of the time 1. 1 into wincer still blew
[2018-04-04 22:40] mtanzer: I think there might still be a few really degenerate ones but not many
[2018-04-04 22:41] mtanzer: like xeno+luci+fuge seems like it's a p1t1 fuge
[2018-04-04 22:41] redrame: fuge will be degenerate no matter what as long as it exists
[2018-04-04 22:41] xyotsuba: I mean at least u need more drones now
[2018-04-04 22:41] mtanzer: yeah fuge was probably the unit that got changed that I'm most worried about
[2018-04-04 22:41] redrame: I'm just happy I could talk some sense into elyot
